prompt: Create a cinematic travel poster for Lisbon using a realistic street photograph style: two vintage yellow-and-red electric trams side by side on sunlit rails, close-up perspective, the left tram cropped large in the foreground and the right tram angled slightly away, with passengers visible through wooden-framed windows, overhead tram wires, parked cars, leafy trees, and bright European city architecture in the background. Add warm late-afternoon sunlight, deep shadows, rich contrast, glossy reflections on glass, and an authentic urban documentary feel. The image contains exactly 2 main trams: 1 foreground tram on the left with a large Coca-Cola-style red side panel, and 1 tram on the right marked with the number 56 near the top front corner. Overlay centered white typography across the lower-middle: a large expressive handwritten brush-script title reading Lisbon, with a smaller all-caps serif tagline beneath reading WHERE EVERY CORNER TELLS A STORY. Include thin decorative horizontal divider lines on both sides of the tagline and a small diamond ornament centered below it. Compose as a polished tourism campaign image, 3:2 landscape aspect ratio, no border, no watermark, keep the text crisp and legible while preserving the realistic photographic background.
